summary: Root mineralization in primary teeth generally concludes at about 6 years; this corresponds to choice C as indicated for completion during early childhood when all tooth structures are fully developed and stabilized. The critical point here pertains specifically to timing related to full dentition establishment before permanent successors erupt. Agent2 reasoned that based on standard developmental timelines, options other than those aligned closely with a child being roughly aged five or more would be incorrect due to their proximity to complete eruption cycles post-early youth stages. This summary captures both core elements needed—age milestone relevance—and contextualizes it within broader pediatric oral health considerations pertinent directly addressing the query posed
